speaker: MATT
raw_text: This is what happens when we're gone for two weeks, we have to cram all of our backup, like, catchup announcements and little friend-bumps there at the front.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They're so cute!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: And my ring of flask. My one ring. Thank you to everybody.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: One scotch to bind them all together.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: It's apple juice! They gave me the flask, not what's in it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And Liam, you said you wanted to--?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Hey, Marvel Animated has started to use me as Doctor Strange, so. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And it's so good!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And also, if you need to put your dice in something, you should go to wyrmwoodgaming.com and use the code 'critrole,' which will get you free shipping.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: You put your dice in the thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Hey, look at this beautiful box.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: They're beautiful. Many of you have been purchasing them. They're lovely to make nice presents for yourself or for another person.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Touché. Well, I'll get about my business here, you said you're traveling for half a day to a day, say meet back at the Slayer's Take in about 24, 36 hours, then head on to the east?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: I put mine out as well and say  your appearance fascinates me on so many levels, I look forward to getting to know you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She pulls her hand away and she goes, "Interesting coming from you, definitely."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "All right."

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: I don't think we met either, how you doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Anyway."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: That's Kash. A man of few words.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I've met few as unremarkable as you in my travels."

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: I'm sorry, I wasn't listening, what?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Are we meeting back in a couple days or are we waiting, what's the business here, I--"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: He just raised his voice a little, that was the difference.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I didn't catch that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I was trying to arrange passage to the east. Got a little bit of pull here in the city, some favors that're owed to me, I figured maybe I could do a favor, but that's, I don't know. If you're going to have an attitude about it."

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Well then we're in trouble, I have an attitude about everything.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Pay him no mind, it's not personal. Kash.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: What? I'm just, it's a logical question, see, she's got something to do in Vasselheim, we have to wait, what does she have to do? I don't feel like I'm talking out of turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Making friends as always.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: To be fair, Kima and I had a rough start, too. She's, I mean, I love you, Kima, but I mean sometimes, you can get a little bit of, there's--

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "I'm assuming a day, I have to try and find a couple of folks and pull some strings."

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Well then we're in trouble, I have an attitude about everything.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Pay him no mind, it's not personal. Kash.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: What? I'm just, it's a logical question, see, she's got something to do in Vasselheim, we have to wait, what does she have to do? I don't feel like I'm talking out of turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Making friends as always.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: To be fair, Kima and I had a rough start, too. She's, I mean, I love you, Kima, but I mean sometimes, you can get a little bit of, there's--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Are we meeting back in a couple days or are we waiting, what's the business here, I--"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How long do you need to get what you have done? You need a day?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As far as lakes go, it's maybe a few hundred feet across. It's more from a part of the land mass actually having a large sinkhole and where certain things may have existed, and there are bits of ruin throughout the surroundings of some sort of a structure that existed there, but most of it seems to have been swallowed up by the ground and the lake has formed in its place, though this was quite some time ago. And it swells or drains based on how much snowfall there has been in recent seasons, and it's been a pretty heavy winter for you guys, so you imagine from the times you've seen it that it's been pretty high, if not frozen over at times.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do you think that this is going to lead to some serious fighting? Because it sounds like these Vestiges are well-guarded.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well, you don't have any reason to think that. Could be hanging from a tree branch.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm sure. You know what, Grog, that's an excellent point.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Just hiding in plain sight.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Hanging on a tree branch under the lake.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's where I'd hide it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Z, Kash, you all have been by the Loch before, would you know what--

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: It was pretty unremarkable last time we were there if it's the one I'm thinking of.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Actually, I may have remembered something about the Loch? What do I, would I have remembered anything at all?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a history check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: What do I do for that?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Roll a 20 and add it to your history. So 11.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: 11. Is that an eight?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's a pretty unremarkable Loch.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: It's a pretty unremarkable Loch.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: (laughs) Oh, it's been two weeks and I missed you guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I wonder if somebody knows about a cavern under the lake.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's what I would assume, is there must be some sort of underground structure, some part of the ruins.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Or maybe we need to breathe under-fricking-water.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I've got two vials of that shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Why don't we take the rest of the night to try and do some research. Is there any way we can find maybe maps or something about ancient history books?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: And I do have a map from the Slayer's Take that I could--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, no, but I mean, we need to find like books about civilizations that might've been here in the past that might get us some sort of-- Like a library. Is there a library?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Pictures or no pictures?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: There might be some pictures.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: All right. I'm in.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is there a library nearby?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: At a fast pace on foot, it would take you seven to eight hours.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Then I think we should go. We can handle it. Yeah, I'm ready.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Keyleth, can't you turn us all to mist, and then we poof there?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I was going to say, do you want to Mist Walk this? It would be appropriate this time.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Then we could be there before the evening.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Can we control the direction that we move?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can you Mist Walk us all, including Trinket?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Context is everything.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Better double-check that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Let me see how many people I can do.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How far away is it? Ish?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You can see a cluster of large eagles in the distance. You guys manage to swoop around. Eventually, as the sun begins to make its way towards the horizon-- you managed to cut a good four or five hours off your journey on this, making your way towards the Marrowglade Loch. There are certain areas where the canopy itself breaks apart, and you can see ravines, you can see where areas of the topography have cracked or shifted in time, and there are drops that go deep under the earth, though you cannot quite see where they would continue to go, until you make your way to the Marrowglade, and there you can see, still full to the top, if a little icy at the surface, it seems relatively undisturbed. You make your way, all drifting down, back beneath the canopy of the Vesper Timberland, and at the edges of the lake.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The t-shirt exists.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: It does. Thanks.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I can imagine as part of the enchantment, as you guys can pass wind through your mist vocal cords.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Just about everything. He's very good at passing wind.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, he's delightful.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You really should just chain up with me and I'll get us there super quick.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so you guys begin to head westward, following your previous experience at the direction that it takes you. You push that way. Kima is also still getting used to this whole mist form endeavor. It takes a little bit for you guys to adjust to this locomotion, but it is very swift in comparison to moving on foot. Drifting over the top of the forest canopies, you guys, riding the top in this full-on, Falcor fly-through moment as you're cruising through the snow-capped forest.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Like E.T.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's awesome.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: (laughs) If a bear could grin, it would. However, the process is very strange as you see this misty cloud (poof) into a bear. It's about a minute for the full transformation. The spell still maintains its eight-hour time period, and you can transform back and forth during that eight-hour period.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 11? Doesn't seem to have any specific elements or markings.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Can I try, as well, because I can read any writing that we find? May I make a check, as well?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an investigation check. Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Oh, that's terrible. Never mind!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So, you guys all rest around the outskirts of this lake.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How do we stop being like this?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Just think about it. Think about being a person again.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How high are we?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As high as you want to be. Right now, everyone has come down to the edge of the lake, so you're right off the ground.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Should we turn like people again?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, let's turn like people again.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Tell Trinket to turn like a bear.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Why is everyone talking like this?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, can we talk normal? Oh.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Trinket, hon. Just concentrate on turning back into a bear, and you'll turn back into a bear.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Okay."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Tell him I love him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Oh! And Vex loves you.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: So we'll say it's been about three to four hours, so you're about half-duration. All of you eventually take a minute to revert back to your physical forms, and you're all now standing at the edge of the lake.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Just think about it. Think about being a person again.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How high are we?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As high as you want to be. Right now, everyone has come down to the edge of the lake, so you're right off the ground.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Should we turn like people again?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, let's turn like people again.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Tell Trinket to turn like a bear.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Why is everyone talking like this?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, can we talk normal? Oh.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Trinket, hon. Just concentrate on turning back into a bear, and you'll turn back into a bear.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Okay."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Tell him I love him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Oh! And Vex loves you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) If a bear could grin, it would. However, the process is very strange as you see this misty cloud (poof) into a bear. It's about a minute for the full transformation. The spell still maintains its eight-hour time period, and you can transform back and forth during that eight-hour period.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Killer.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Yeah. It takes you a few minutes, but you manage to break apart and make a head-sized hole.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Or we'll all drown.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Or we'll drown.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: It's a freshwater loch, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I don't know. I walk up to the edge of the loch and I look in the water. What does the water look like?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's ice.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So your head goes (boosh). A little crack. (cracking noise)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's ice!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As he says that, you see a trickle of crimson streaking down the front of his head.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you, Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Welcome!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to put some pressure on the ice with my foot. Is it giving, or is it not giving?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At the place where he put his head, or any other place?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No, I'm walking. I'm just testing the ice.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Testing it with your foot on the edge, it doesn't seem to be any give. Looking up close, you can see it's probably a good inch and a half of ice.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I go hit the place where Grog hit his head and see if I can crack a hole in the ice?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's past sunset. It's now in proper night.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Could be a chimney.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Unlikely, but it could be a chimney.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Working for the Slayer's Take, and having done research on the animals that we have destroyed for money, would there be anything that we would know about serpents, or another creature that might inhabit this loch?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Serpents in lakes? Nothing that would be monumental beyond actual water serpents, which are minor inconveniences and just natural creatures. Anything of this size, per se-- there are all sorts of entities that it could be, but none that would be specific to a lake location, necessarily. Whether it be a subterranean creature that uses it for passage, or if there's something that lives in the lake, or is part of the lake, living in the water itself, nothing comes to mind, from your experience.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What time is it?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right. You guys go to sleep. The two of you stay up. All right. You watch and the next four hours go by, unhindered.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit. Really? You know. Those ones I have.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a dexterity check. Just a straight dexterity check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Where did you get those thieves' tools?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I have thieves' tools because I'm a thief now.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Where did you get them? Did you buy them?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I stole them, dude.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Stole them from where? When? I don't remember that. Did that happen in-game, or did you make that up?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Matt told me I have thieves' tools! Dick. 27. 27 dexterity.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He is asleep. Talking in his sleep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Voice in my head. Brother. Twin.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Shut up!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You finish out the rest of your watch. Using the moonlight at your disposal, you manage to make a couple of owlbear arrows with the feathers you found.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: These are special.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They're functional. They'll do.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: They're fucking feathers.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Shut up. I'm writing, "two owlbear arrows."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Next watch?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Hello.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: At this point, you hear a distant screech that echoes across the night sky. All of you wake up. While not extremely loud, it is very noticeable and cuts through the silence, as there is a very still wind tonight.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Never.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We're a bit of an awkward family, but we are trying.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: And I apologize for any of the mistakes I've made, and honestly any of the mistakes I'm likely to make, but I am starting to imagine a better version of myself. I'm not quite sure who that is yet, but there is a destiny, I think, for us all together.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: This is all a lot bigger than us individually now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We've been given mighty deeds to accomplish. We will rise. Or if we fail, no one will ever know. (chuckles)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) There's comfort in that. All right. I've said my piece.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At which point, you hear a voice say, "Good. I'm trying to sleep." (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The clinking of armor as Kima rolls over on one of the branches and is face-palming at the conversation.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You are really fucking cool. You know that, don't you?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As the hours pass-- (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: That's amazing. It's a skill that I've never had before. But I guess as you said, when you really take in the world and can see people and how they feel, you can really write a powerful ballad.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm a man of the world, I'm well-traveled, I'm cultured--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (snorts awake) Rickshaw, rickshaw-- (snores)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Rickshaw's a good one! Let me tell you, secret weapon. I sleep near Percy a lot and he rhymes in his sleep. It's the strangest thing. Here, I'll show you, I'll show you. I haven't a fear in the world!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (mutters incomprehensibly) (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sometimes it takes a while.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: I don't. I don't understand.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It takes a while.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (mumbles) Something rhymes with world.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: See? It's amazing. Every time. Every time.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Absolutely incredible.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Two weeks, folks. We've been storing this up. All right. So what's the plan, guys?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Hm, my ears feel really hot this morning, I don't know why that is.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I've been breathing on them.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What about the little hole in the lake that we smashed? Is it still there, or did it freeze over?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The hole's still there, but there is a very thin layer of what appears to be forming ice that could be easily cracked.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I got it, I'll do that. And I go and take my morning wee right on the little hole where it's starting to freeze back over.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There you go. Grog has now effectively peed in the Marrowglade Loch.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Kash walks over to also pee and looks down at Grog and goes, "Holy god." And just turns and walks. (laughter)

raw_text: 11. Okay. At this point, the dark female form that drifts from the altar, you glancing up intently, feeling the sudden intense, fearful gaze of Vesh upon you. As it gets closer and closer, the presence is different. What you feared was Vesh is not Vesh, actually. It is an image, a specter of the Raven Queen herself. The face is formless at this proximity. You can't make any details. It's just this perpetually blurred female form of a face. The hair just tumbles down past, and as it steps forward, no expression, no words. The hand reaches out towards you, Vax. There's a nod. The magic strengthens, focusing through your moon crystal into Vax's body. I'll take a picture of this so that no one questions this. A moment passes. A second moment passes. The vision of the Raven Queen nods the head to you, steps backward, and the specter vanishes. (gasping) Breath fills Vex's lungs as she begins to cough, her form drifting back to the stonework. Your vision clears, and you find yourself looking up at the partially broken stone ceiling of the dark altar into the face of your brother, tears streaming down his face, the rest of your friends gathered around.

It is the history of her penance for ascending and taking the throne of another entity that was supplanted. It's just seemingly a history tale that is woven in with a very, very strong enchantment, and you see within each one of these little runic sections, there are mentions of elements: fire, ice, lightning, you know, it runs through all these various elemental arcane properties that are bound into this enchantment. You're not entirely certain what the nature of it is.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: I can hold up to ten pounds.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. It's full leather armor. You can just-- I'd say--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I can assist, I've got Mage Hand as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Look, I'm a giant tank and I've got a super long, like, sword, I could try and give it a pick-up if everybody backs away.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Well, what if I lifted it up and put it onto your sword, and then you could carry it out that way?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I was going to put it in the Bag of Holding.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Oh, put it in the Bag of Holding? Can I lift it up and put it in the Bag of Holding?

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Again, can we read anything that's written on it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Well, we need it, that's what's going to help you guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, all the runes are inscribed there; you can make an arcana check. Or religion check, one of the two, probably would.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: I glance at it, I can read. I have Eyes of the Runekeeper.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You'd probably have to take some time with it, like a short rest, to go ahead and ascertain the nature of it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Let's take it!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: All right, what about the jewelry and the black crystal?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can take the black crystal as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It was the runic things in the sarcophagus that set off that blast, not the leather itself?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You don't know. But there were two runic circles within the inside of the sarcophagus that were not visible when you first looked into it, that are both now visible and expended of energy.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: All right, yeah, let's put it in the Bag of Holding. You want to Mage Hand onto my swordy, and then we shish-kabob it into the bag?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I'll take the crystal as well. Is that the only thing-- the rest are all tarnished and green, yes?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: All right. So here's the black-- can I do an investigation check on the crystal at all, or--?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Well played, druid. Well played." And she charges through eagerly, and you guys all follow suit out of the Sun Tree in Whitestone and find yourselves a comfortable bed to sleep for the evening. And that's where we'll call tonight's game. I know it was long, but also, due to scheduling shifts and, y'know, Will couldn't stay for the next week, I didn't want to have to leave in the middle of that, I wanted to give you guys closure to the arc. So thank you for staying for a long session.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Just-- it'd be great, yeah. It's big. It's a big, big world.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "And, just to put this out there, and I don't mean to say this in any selfish way, but I'm really sick of sleeping in trees. Do you have one of those tree travel things you have already to Whitestone? I could really use a bed."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sure, yeah. Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We could all head that way, actually.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: To Whitestone?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do we need to go back, I don't know, sleep for the night?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: I think we could all use some rest, don't you?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We still have the Fire Ashari.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We do still have the Fire Ashari. Granted, I could theoretically just tree back. You guys want to go take a nap at Whitestone?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Could use a bed.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Great!"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Tree Stride.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As the tree opens-- Teleport via Plants. As the tree splits open, showing the portal, Kima, whose eyes light up, looks over. "That--"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: See? Yeah.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: No no no, no, Raise Dead is a proper, like, resurrection-type spell. It just is really expensive, and it's a ritual, there are limitations to it. Revivify is good, but it has to be used within a minute. Which means when you used previously before and we were in mid-combat, and I didn't want to do the whole frickin' ritual in the middle of the battle, so I let it go through.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He survived, he didn't even go below zero on that hit. I was just already at 41, so it lowered me. He had four hit points left.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The best part of that was that you were totally out of the blast range, and you were like, "I want to go be there when they open up the sarcophagus." (explosion)

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Yeah! She ran back! Totally!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I know! No, believe me, I am aware that my greed fucking did that to me. I am aware.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Life lesson.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: And will she learn from this moment?

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: I thought for a second I hadn't written down that spell, I was like, "Oh my god, wait, I didn't write, oh, yes, there it is, thank god." Oh my god.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You still could've done Raise Dead, it just would've been a little more expensive.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: That's crazy, though--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Well, I thought Raise Dead just brought them back, like, to life as a zombie, that's what I thought.
